title: Falaknuma Palace
tagline: Falaknuma Palace — heritage city of Telangana

overview.short: Falaknuma Palace is a palace and a luxury hotel in Hyderabad, Telangana, India.
overview.description: Falaknuma Palace is a palace and a luxury hotel in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. It originally belonged to the Paigah family, and was later owned by the Nizam of Hyderabad. It is on a hillock and covers a 13-hectare (32-acre) area in Falaknuma, 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) from Charminar. Built by Nawab Sir Viqar-ul-Umra, Prime Minister of Hyderabad state and the uncle and brother-in-law of the sixth Nizam.

topPlaces.name: Jahanuma
topPlaces.description: Jahanuma is a neighbourhood in Hyderabad, Telangana. A light cavalry unit of the Paigahs known as Jahanuma Lancers were once located here.

topPlaces.name: Uppuguda
topPlaces.description: Uppuguda is a part of the Old City, Hyderabad, Hyderabad, India.

topPlaces.name: Aliabad, Hyderabad
topPlaces.description: Aliabad is one of the old neighbourhoods in Hyderabad, India. It is part of the old city of Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Shah Ghouse Café
topPlaces.description: Shah Ghouse Café is a popular restaurant chain specializing in Hyderabadi cuisine, located in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. The café is widely celebrated for its biryani, Hyderabadi haleem, and other traditional dishes.

topPlaces.name: Chandrayan Gutta
topPlaces.description: Chandrayana Gutta is a neighbourhood in Old City, Hyderabad, Telangana, India.

topPlaces.name: Hyderabad International Film Festival
topPlaces.description: The Hyderabad International Film Festival (HIFF) is a film festival in Hyderabad, India. The first Hyderabad International Film Festival (HIFF) was held in Hyderabad, Andhra Pradesh, India in 2007.

topPlaces.name: Shah-Ali-Banda
topPlaces.description: Shah Ali Banda is a neighbourhood in Hyderabad, India. It forms a part of the Old City, Hyderabad in an area located close to Charminar.

topPlaces.name: Kings Colony, Shastripuram
topPlaces.description: Shastripuram Housing Colony is an area in the Ranga Reddy District of Telangana, India. It comes under Rajendra Nagar Circle 6 Government of Telangana.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Falaknuma Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Falaknuma Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Falaknuma Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (11 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Falaknuma Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Falaknuma Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Falaknuma Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Falaknuma Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Falaknuma Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jahanuma, Uppuguda, Aliabad, Hyderabad, Shah Ghouse Café, Chandrayan Gutta.
